scope: /Users/ludwig/workspace/st4ck (Talos multi-env infra: Make + OpenTofu + Flux + Woodpecker CI)

clusters.label: k8s-day1-stacks (sequential CNI -> PKI -> ... -> Flux)
clusters.recommended_commis_hint: split across 2 commis - cluster too sequentialized; one commis pre-CNI/PKI, another for identity/security/storage

boundary_functions.name: make k8s-up
boundary_functions.warning: any commis touching this Make target pulls in two clusters; sequence the work

boundary_functions.name: stacks/storage (k8s-storage-apply)
boundary_functions.reason: TF-managed at day1, but its HelmReleases are also referenced from cluster-4 (Flux); splits between cluster-2 and cluster-4
boundary_functions.warning: ownership ambiguous: storage Helm releases are deployed by Tofu AND referenced from clusters/management/kustomization.yaml

boundary_functions.name: make scaleway-up
boundary_functions.warning: biggest cross-cluster bridge in the build

god_functions.name: Makefile (root, 873 LOC)
god_functions.evidence: 873 lines, ~80 targets, 16 stack paths defined inline, calls itself 18 times via $(MAKE) recursion (k8s-up, k8s-down, kaas-up, kaas-down, scaleway-nuke, upgrade, local-up, local-down)

god_functions.name: envs/scaleway/main.tf (386 LOC)
god_functions.evidence: 19 resource/data/module blocks in a single file; talos secrets + machine config + scaleway VMs + bootstrap + kubeconfig all colocated

god_functions.name: stacks/identity/main.tf (288 LOC)
god_functions.evidence: 11 resources covering CNPG operator + PostgreSQL cluster + ScheduledBackup + Hydra TLS + Kratos + Hydra + OIDC client + Pomerium; deepest local depends_on chain (5 levels)

god_functions.name: stacks/storage/main.tf (259 LOC)
god_functions.evidence: garage_wait + garage_layout + garage_buckets_keys local-exec chain blurs declarative/imperative boundary

shotgun_surgery.trigger: rename or move a single stack folder under stacks/
files_touched: Makefile (TF_<NAME>, STATE_<NAME>, k8s-<name>-init/apply/destroy targets, k8s-up, k8s-down, STACKS list)
shotgun_surgery.remediation: introduce a stacks-manifest.yaml read by Make + Woodpecker + Flux kustomization; or a make_stack($1) macro that emits init/apply/destroy targets from a single STACKS list

files_touched: Makefile (paths + targets + STATE_ + k8s-up + k8s-down + STACKS + k8s-init)
shotgun_surgery.remediation: scaffold script: bin/new-stack <name> generates the 4 boilerplate touchpoints from a template

anti_patterns.name: God Function (Makefile)
anti_patterns.evidence: Makefile 873 LOC, 80+ targets, recursive $(MAKE) self-calls 18 times; god_score 0.82

anti_patterns.name: Shotgun Surgery (stack list duplication)
anti_patterns.evidence: the canonical list of stacks appears 4 times: Makefile $(STACKS), Makefile k8s-up sequence, .woodpecker.yml deploy-* chain, clusters/management/kustomization.yaml

anti_patterns.name: Hub and Spoke (vault-backend)
anti_patterns.evidence: every TF stack init-targets vault-backend at :8080 via $(call tf_init); single point of failure for ALL state operations (16 stacks, 9 STATE_* paths). Documented as 'must run BEFORE any tofu command' in CLAUDE.md.

anti_patterns.name: Layering Violation (managed-cluster -> envs/scaleway/image)
anti_patterns.evidence: managed-cluster-apply target reads envs/scaleway/image output via $(TF) -chdir=$(TF_SCW_IMAGE) output -raw image_name from inside a stacks/ Make recipe; data layer (image build) is read by an upper layer (tenant cluster) via shell-out

anti_patterns.name: Feature Envy (Makefile -> envs/scaleway/iam)
anti_patterns.evidence: $(call scw_iam_out,...) shells out to tofu output 7 times to retrieve project_id, image AK/SK, cluster AK/SK, ci AK/SK; every invocation re-runs tofu in scw_iam dir

anti_patterns.name: Copy-Paste Cluster (k8s-<stack>-init/apply/destroy)
anti_patterns.evidence: 11 stacks each define near-identical init/apply/destroy triples (cni, monitoring, pki, identity, security, storage, flux-bootstrap, capi, kamaji, autoscaling, gateway-api); same 3-line body, only TF_DIR + STATE_ vary; ~110 lines of structural copy

bottlenecks.evidence: deploy-cluster -> wait-api -> deploy-cni -> deploy-pki -> deploy-monitoring -> deploy-identity -> deploy-security -> deploy-storage -> deploy-flux all gated on build-image; cumulative wait ~15 min image build + 8 stack deploys

parallelism_opportunities: deploy-monitoring is independent from deploy-pki at the K8s level (different namespaces, no shared CRD); chain is sequentialized for race-condition reasons (CLAUDE.md acknowledges this), but monitoring + security could fan out
validate runs all stacks serially in one step; could matrix-fan-out to 11 parallel validations

verdict: Acceptable - well-structured for an IaC repo; main tangle is the canonical-stack-list duplication and the Makefile size.
